summary.title: Summary
summary.body:
## Summary

This file defines format choices across SocratexPipeline project types.

code_projects.title: Code Projects
code_projects.body:
## Code Projects

Code projects should use JSON for standardized project memory, registries, configuration, indexes, diagnostics, and AI-readable structured documents.

Use Markdown in code projects only for scratch-style files:

- raw user instruction intake,
- temporary notes,
- rough drafts,
- human scratchpads,
- short README-style user-facing docs when no structured state is stored.

Preferred code-project formats:

- `DOCS.json`
- `STATE.json`
- `_PLAN.json`
- `TODO.json`
- `BUGS.json`
- `BUGS-SOLVED.json`
- `CHANGELOG.json` when `changelog.enabled` is not `no`
- `DECISIONS.json`
- `PIPELINE-CONFIG.json`
- `context-docs/*.json`
- generated indexes or caches as `.json`
- diagnostics summaries as `.json` when machine-read, `.json` when agent-read

non_code_projects.title: Non-Code Projects
non_code_projects.body:
## Non-Code Projects

Non-code projects should use Markdown for files the user is expected to read or edit directly.

Use Markdown for:

- forced agent entrypoints such as `SOCRATEX.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, or provider-specific instruction files,
- user scratch files and raw prompt intake,
- user-facing non-code project memory such as `STATE.md`, `_PLAN.md`, `BACKLOG.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `ISSUES.md`, `JOURNAL.md`, and `REVIEW.md`,
- final prose artifacts and public documentation.

Use JSON for files managed only by the agent:

- `DOCS.json`,
- `PIPELINE-CONFIG.json`,
- `_INSTRUCTION-QUEUE.json`,
- generated caches and indexes,
- agent-only `context-docs/*.json`,
- large AI-only internal memory files,
- machine-readable diagnostics or summaries.

If both the user and the agent read a file, prefer Markdown in non-code projects unless the user explicitly wants structured JSON.

migration_rule.title: Migration Rule
migration_rule.body:
## Migration Rule

When converting a project from Markdown to JSON:

1. Preserve all unresolved requirements.
2. Convert agent-only structured registries first.
3. Keep forced entrypoints, user scratches, and non-code user-facing memory as Markdown.
4. Update the audit rules and initializer together.
